title: Create LLD-specific 5-step guided tour using existing OnboardingOverlay infrastructure
description: ONBOARDING AUDIT: Platform has a 5-step OnboardingOverlay (onboarding-overlay.tsx) with spotlight mask, step navigation, and data-onboarding targeting. But ALL 5 steps are System Design-specific ('Drag Components', 'Connect Nodes', 'Run Simulation'). When user enters LLD module, NO tour fires. Create an LLD-specific tour: (1) Welcome to LLD Studio, (2) Patterns sidebar, (3) UML canvas explanation, (4) Properties panel, (5) SOLID principles tab. Use existing OnboardingOverlay infrastructure — just define LLD_STEPS array and trigger on first LLD visit.
acceptanceCriteria: LLD-specific tour fires on first visit to LLD module
5 steps: Welcome, Patterns sidebar, Canvas/UML explanation, Properties panel, SOLID tab
Steps use data-onboarding attributes on LLD UI elements
Tour auto-loads Observer pattern at step 3 to show a real diagram
Skip button always visible. 'Replay' accessible from settings.
Tour stored in localStorage separate from System Design tour
Mobile-compatible: steps adapt to bottom sheet layout

title: Add contextual first-encounter tooltips for LLD-specific features
description: ONBOARDING AUDIT: ContextualTooltip component exists with useFirstEncounter hook but CONTEXTUAL_FEATURES array has zero LLD entries. Add: (1) 'Pattern Browser' tooltip on first sidebar view, (2) 'Before/After Toggle' tooltip when SOLID demo first loads, (3) 'Code Generation' tooltip when user first views generated code, (4) 'Code to Diagram' tooltip on Code tab first click. Each shows once per feature via localStorage.
acceptanceCriteria: 4 LLD-specific entries added to CONTEXTUAL_FEATURES array
Each tooltip shows once on first encounter of the feature
Tooltips use existing ContextualTooltip component with motion animation
Tooltips point to correct LLD UI elements (sidebar tabs, canvas, properties)
Dismissible with X button, never shown again after dismissal

title: Rewrite all 5 LLD empty states with actionable CTA buttons and curiosity hooks
description: ONBOARDING AUDIT: All 5 empty states follow the same anti-pattern: '[Do X from the sidebar]' as plain text with zero buttons. Rewrite: (1) Canvas: 'Ready to explore? Start with the Observer pattern — the most useful pattern you'll ever learn.' + 'Load Observer Pattern →' button, (2) Properties: 'Click any class to see its details.' (contextual, no CTA needed), (3) Bottom: 'Pattern explanations and code appear here.' + 'Try Behavioral Simulator →' button, (4) Sequence: 'See how services communicate.' + 'Load HTTP Request Flow →', (5) State Machine: 'Watch a system change states.' + 'Load Order Lifecycle →'
acceptanceCriteria: All 5 empty states have descriptive text + curiosity hook
Canvas empty state has 'Load Observer Pattern →' CTA button that calls handleSelectPattern
Sequence empty state has 'Load HTTP Request →' CTA
State Machine empty state has 'Load Order Lifecycle →' CTA
CTA buttons styled as primary action (bg-primary text-primary-foreground)
Empty states show a small preview illustration or icon relevant to the content type

title: Add beginner-friendly jargon tooltips for UML terminology in LLD module
description: ONBOARDING AUDIT: Complete beginners encounter jargon immediately: 'CREATIONAL', 'STRUCTURAL', 'BEHAVIORAL', 'composition', 'aggregation', 'realization', '«interface»', '«abstract»', visibility symbols (+, -, #, ~). None of these are explained. Add hover tooltips on: (1) Category headers explaining each GoF category in plain English, (2) Relationship type labels in the legend, (3) Stereotype badges on class boxes, (4) Visibility symbols in attribute/method lists. Use the existing Tooltip component from @/components/ui/tooltip.
acceptanceCriteria: Category headers (CREATIONAL, STRUCTURAL, BEHAVIORAL, MODERN) have tooltips with 1-sentence plain-English explanation
Legend relationship types (inheritance, composition, etc.) have tooltips explaining what each means
Stereotype badges («interface», «abstract», «enumeration») have tooltips
Visibility symbols (+, -, #, ~) have tooltips: '+ = public', '- = private', '# = protected', '~ = package'
All tooltips use existing Tooltip/TooltipContent components

title: Add idle detection — prompt help after 20s of inactivity in LLD module
description: ONBOARDING AUDIT: Zero stuck detection. A confused user staring at the empty canvas for 30 seconds gets zero help. Add: track last user interaction timestamp. After 20s of inactivity on empty canvas, show a subtle animated prompt: 'Not sure where to start? Try clicking Observer in the sidebar →' with an arrow pointing at the sidebar. After 30s on a loaded pattern with no clicks, show: 'Click any class box to see its details →'. Non-intrusive — just a floating hint that fades after 5s or on any click.
acceptanceCriteria: After 20s of inactivity on empty canvas, floating hint appears suggesting a pattern to click
After 30s of inactivity on a loaded pattern, hint suggests clicking a class box
Hints are non-modal — subtle floating card at bottom of canvas
Hints auto-dismiss after 5 seconds or on any user interaction
Hints only show once per session (don't repeat on subsequent idle periods)
No hints when user is actively exploring (resets on every click/key)

title: Add 'Related Patterns' links to every pattern — refactoring.guru parity
description: COMPLETENESS GATE: refactoring.guru shows 'Relations with Other Patterns' per pattern (e.g., Observer page links to Mediator, Command, Chain of Responsibility with explanation of how they relate). Our patterns have cross-references via 'relatedPatterns' field on DesignPattern type: array of { patternId: string, relationship: string } where relationship explains HOW they're related. Display in Properties panel under 'Related Patterns' section with clickable links.
acceptanceCriteria: DesignPattern type extended with relatedPatterns: { patternId: string; relationship: string }[]
All 34 patterns have 2-4 related pattern links each
Properties panel shows 'Related Patterns' section with clickable links
Clicking a related pattern loads it on canvas immediately
Relationship text explains HOW the patterns relate (not just 'see also')
Matches refactoring.guru's 'Relations with Other Patterns' depth

title: Wire AI explanation feature for LLD patterns using existing ai-store infrastructure
description: COMPLETENESS GATE: Platform has ai-store.ts (6.3K) with API key management and HintPanel.tsx for Interview module, but LLD has ZERO AI integration. Add 'Ask AI' button per pattern that sends the pattern's UML + description to the AI and returns a contextual explanation. E.g., 'Why is Observer better than direct method calls here?' Uses existing AI infrastructure — just needs LLD-specific prompting.
acceptanceCriteria: Ask AI button visible in Properties panel when a pattern is loaded
Clicking sends pattern context (name, description, classes, relationships) to AI
AI response displayed in a drawer/panel below the pattern description
Uses existing ai-store.ts API key management
Works only when user has configured an API key (graceful fallback otherwise)
3 preset questions: 'When to use this?', 'Common mistakes?', 'Real-world example?'
